executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware crisis with 103 Canadian organizations compromised in the past 48 hours across all critical sectors. High-profile victims include Canada Goose, National Money Mart (database leaked), Peterborough Public Health, Eastern Townships School Board, Municipality of North Perth, and multiple law firms. The Qilin ransomware group leads the assault with 21 victims, followed by SafePay (14), Play (13), and CLOP (13). This coordinated wave targets manufacturing, healthcare, education, public sector, and financial services simultaneously—indicating a systematic campaign against Canadian infrastructure.

items.name: Qilin (Agenda)
items.targets: 21 Canadian organizations including Service Star Freightways, Kuzco Lighting, Waterloo Information Systems, A Lococo Wholesale, Traffic Tech, Gradient Wind Engineering, Workers Health & Safety Centre, Bergmanis Preyra (law firm), Pre-Con Builders, Questica, Eastern Townships School Board, MG Chartered Professional Accountant, Watermark Beach Resort, CST Coal, BioPharma Services, NovAtel (Hexagon subsidiary), Maheu&Maheu, Berts Electric, and Capp Shupak
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service), T1078 (Valid Accounts)

items.name: SafePay
items.targets: 14 Canadian organizations including briwaycarriers.com, mattandsteve.com (agriculture), ripobec.com (agriculture), groupepiche.ca (construction), precisionaluminum.ca (manufacturing), kenalex.ca (construction), silverlinegroupinc.com (construction), teccart.qc.ca (education), and mactavishco.ca
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery)

items.name: Play
items.targets: 13 Canadian organizations including Select Tool, Equine Canada, Chemical Computing Group, Paisley Products of Canada, Makivik, ALLMAX, Pewarchuk CPA, Security ONE Alarm Systems, Katch Kan, and Turkstra Trusses
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1078 (Valid Accounts), T1083 (File and Directory Discovery)

items.name: CLOP (Cl0p)
items.targets: 13 Canadian organizations including STRATEGICOBJECTIVES.COM, IDEALWELDERS.COM, CONWEST.COM, CMHHELI.COM (helicopter transport), GRANITEPOINTE.CA (hospitality), SMTLAWYERS.CA (law firm), CLOUDDATAWORKS.CA, SENSIBLESOLUTIONS.CA, WHISKIJACKRESORTS.COM, CCCM.BC.CA, TECHNIKEL.CA, MODTECH.CA, EASTPLATS.COM (energy), and CENTAURPRODUCTS.COM

canada_landscape.summary: Canada faces a critical ransomware emergency with 103 organizations compromised across all sectors in 48 hours. Notable victims include major retailer Canada Goose, financial institution National Money Mart with confirmed database leak, public health authority Peterborough Public Health, Eastern Townships School Board affecting students, and Municipality of North Perth disrupting municipal services. No CCCS advisories were identified in this reporting period despite the scale of attacks.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose compromised by CoinbaseCartel ransomware gang; Active Green + Ross (automotive retail) hit by Sinobi
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company database leaked by Everest ransomware; Morison Insurance Brokers compromised by Sinobi; Trucash (financial services) hit by Lynx; multiple accounting firms (Pewarchuk CPA, MG Chartered Professional Accountant) compromised; law firms SMTLAWYERS.CA (CLOP), Bergmanis Preyra (Qilin), and unnamed firm (Anubis) breached

report_2w.executive_summary: A GitHub internal repository security incident has triggered a Canadian government advisory (CCCS AL26-013), urging organizations to review their GitHub configurations immediately. Attackers are actively exploiting CVE-2026-35616 in FortiClient EMS to deploy credential-stealing malware, and CVE-2026-39987 in Marimo is being leveraged with AI-powered post-exploitation tools. Major U.S. breaches at Charter Communications (4.9M accounts) and Carnival (6M customers) by the ShinyHunters group highlight ongoing data theft campaigns. A sophisticated npm supply chain attack is targeting cloud and CI/CD secrets through typosquatted packages.

items.description: Authentication bypass in FortiClient EMS leading to infostealer deployment
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: Medium

items.description: Pre-authentication remote code execution in Marimo ≤0.20.4 used with LLM agent for post-exploitation
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

items.name: ShinyHunters
items.targets: Charter Communications (4.9M accounts), Carnival (6M customers), BCD Travel
items.ttps: T1078 Valid Accounts, T1530 Data from Cloud Storage Object, T1567 Exfiltration Over Web Service, T1486 Data Encrypted for Impact, extortion campaigns following data theft

items.name: Silent Ransom Group
items.targets: Multiple organizations via in-person IT impersonation
items.ttps: T1589 Gather Victim Identity Information, T1598 Phishing for Information, T1204 User Execution, physical access social engineering, in-person impersonation of IT staff to gain initial access

items.name: The Gentlemen Ransomware (Storm-2XXX)
items.targets: Multiple organizations with self-propagating Go encryptor
items.ttps: T1486 Data Encrypted for Impact, T1210 Exploitation of Remote Services, T1570 Lateral Tool Transfer, self-propagation capabilities, RaaS operation, data posted to BreachForums

items.name: Akira Ransomware
items.targets: Schacht Law Office, Interstate Roofing
items.ttps: T1486 Data Encrypted for Impact, T1490 Inhibit System Recovery, T1567 Exfiltration Over Web Service, double extortion tactics

items.name: Qilin Ransomware
items.targets: 17 Australian organizations in 2026, data published after attacks
items.ttps: T1486 Data Encrypted for Impact, T1490 Inhibit System Recovery, T1567 Exfiltration Over Web Service, targeting Australian healthcare and infrastructure

items.name: Chinese APT (Iran War-themed campaign)
items.targets: Maritime and energy companies exploiting Iran conflict for lures
items.ttps: T1566 Phishing, T1204 User Execution, T1078 Valid Accounts, geopolitical event exploitation for targeting critical infrastructure

items.name: SideCopy APT
items.targets: Ministry of Finance Afghanistan via XenoRAT deployment
items.ttps: T1566 Spearphishing Attachment, T1204 User Execution, T1059 Command and Scripting Interpreter, Pakistan-linked targeting of Afghan government

items.name: NPM Typosquatting Campaign (forge-jsxy and others)
items.targets: Cloud environments, CI/CD pipelines, crypto wallets via 12+ malicious npm packages
items.ttps: T1195.002 Compromise Software Supply Chain, T1539 Steal Web Session Cookie, T1552.001 Credentials In Files, T1567 Exfiltration Over Web Service, targeting AWS credentials and CI/CD secrets, 22 versions pushed in 22 days

items.name: DragonForce Ransomware
items.targets: Pieralisi MAIP S.P.A. (Italy, mechanical industry)
items.ttps: T1486 Data Encrypted for Impact, T1490 Inhibit System Recovery, T1567 Exfiltration Over Web Service

items.name: Everest Ransomware
items.targets: Ito Group LLP (Social Security number breach)
items.ttps: T1486 Data Encrypted for Impact, T1490 Inhibit System Recovery, T1567 Exfiltration Over Web Service, T1078 Valid Accounts

items.name: GlassWorm Malware Infrastructure
items.targets: Multiple organizations via npm supply chain
items.ttps: T1195.002 Compromise Software Supply Chain, T1071 Application Layer Protocol, infrastructure dismantled by CrowdStrike, Google, Shadowserver

canada_landscape.summary: The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (CCCS) issued Alert AL26-013 concerning a security incident impacting GitHub internal repositories, urging organizations to review their GitHub Enterprise configurations and access controls immediately. No specific breaches of major Canadian retailers or financial institutions were identified in this reporting period, though the GitHub advisory has national security implications for government and enterprise users.
